title: 2026-27 Departmental results framework and Program inventory: Health Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## 2026-27 Departmental results framework and Program inventory: Health Canada
Health Canada's approved Departmental results framework and Program inventory for 2026-27 are as follows:

## Departmental results framework

### Core Responsibility 1: Health Care Systems
Result 1: Canada has modern and sustainable health care systems
- Indicator 1: Real per capita health expenditure
- Indicator 2: Pharmaceutical spending per capita
- Indicator 3: Percentage of family health service providers and other health professionals that can share patient health information electronically
- Indicator 4: Percentage of Canadians that can access their own comprehensive health record electronically
- Indicator 5: Percentage of Canada Health Act compliance issues addressed within 24 months of identification
Result 2: Canadians have access to appropriate and effective health services
- Indicator 6: Percentage of Canadians with a mental disorder who have expressed that they have an unmet mental health care need
- Indicator 7: Percentage of Canadians who have expressed that they have an unmet need for access to home care services
- Indicator 8: Percentage of Canadians who take less medication than prescribed because of the cost
- Indicator 9: Percentage of the Canadian target population that is deemed eligible after applying for the Canadian Dental Care Plan
- Indicator 10: Percentage of providers actively participating in the Canadian Dental Care Plan
- Indicator 11: Percentage of Canadians reporting access to a regular health care provider

### Core Responsibility 2: Health Protection and Promotion
Result 3: Canadians have access to health products that meet safety, effectiveness, and quality requirements
- Indicator 12: Percentage of human new drug decisions issued within service standards
- Indicator 13: Percentage of domestic drug companies deemed to be compliant with manufacturing requirements under the Food and Drugs Act and associated Regulations
Result 4: Canadians are protected from unsafe consumer and commercial products and substances
- Indicator 14: Percentage of all compliance actions for consumer products and cosmetics that were carried out voluntarily by industry in cooperation with Health Canada
- Indicator 15: Percentage of actions taken in a timely manner to protect the health of people in Canada from chemicals found to be a risk to human health
- Indicator 16: Percentage of pre-market pesticide submission reviews that are completed within service standards
Result 5: Canadians make healthy choices
- Indicator 17: Percentage of Canadians (aged 18+) who are currently smoking cigarettes
- Indicator 18: Percentage of youth (grades 10-12) who report frequent (daily to weekly) cannabis use in the past 30 days
- Indicator 19: Percentage of Canadians who use dietary guidance provided by Health Canada
Internal services are the services that are provided within a department so that it can meet its corporate obligations and deliver its programs.

### Core Responsibility 1: Health Care Systems
- Program 1: Responsive Health Care Systems
- Program 2: Healthy People and Communities
- Program 3: Quality Health Science, Data and Evidence
- Program 4: Oral Health

### Core Responsibility 2: Health Protection and Promotion
- Program 5: Pharmaceutical Drugs
- Program 6: Biologic and Radiopharmaceutical Drugs
- Program 7: Medical Devices
- Program 8: Natural Health Products
- Program 9: Food and Nutrition
- Program 10: Air Quality
- Program 11: Climate Change
- Program 12: Water Quality
- Program 13: Health Impacts of Chemicals
- Program 14: Consumer Product Safety
- Program 15: Workplace Hazardous Products
- Program 16: Tobacco Control
- Program 17: Controlled Substances
- Program 18: Cannabis
- Program 19: Radiation Protection
- Program 20: Pesticides
- Program 21: Health Canada Specialized Services
